blocks.text: 1 We’ve expanded the cafe with over 200 additional seats. This special event is for our up and coming skiers and riders to get a taste of what it’s like to compete in freeride. Hosted by our ambassadors. 2 Increased snowmaking capacity in the Terrain Parks. 3 Two new groomers join the fleet, ramping 13 SEPTEMBER up our grooming power.
A gathering of skiers and snowboarders in The Stash 4 Fire pits are coming to the café deck, enjoying a custom park along with music, a BBQ and bringing new ambience. spot prizes.

Increased Ski Bus capacity with the purchase of 3 6 new MAN 4WD coaches and a 4th on delivery mid- SNOW MACHINE season. It’s time to party on the slopes with Snow Machine – the best vibe around, great music and a whole lot of fun. New Snowsports Heli Premium Private experience, 7 where guests can enjoy a day heliskiing with one of our experienced Snowsports coaches. theremarkables.co.nz/events

The Remarkables ski area is where you’ll find your freedom. Located in the adventure capital of the world - Queenstown, New Zealand - it’s not hard to see why this place is so addictive. Our mountain is a welcoming, adventurous ski area boasting three sun-drenched, north-facing bowls and an epic terrain park that includes an iconic Burton Stash. Known for being one of the best mountains for progression, the ski area offers a range of terrain to suit everyone. From off-piste black runs and daring chutes to wide-open learner slopes and everything in between. The iconic mountain is the Home of Freeride in New Zealand and has hosted the country’s only World Freeride Qualifier events. The Remarkables is also home to the Southern Hemisphere’s only Freeride World Tour Academy. Since 2014 strong investment has seen the development of an extensive snowmaking set up, installation of three high speed detachable chairlifts and an award-winning base building – all of which contribute to the world-class facilities on mountain. Along with sister mountains, Coronet Peak and Mt Hutt, The Remarkables is dedicated to Kaitiakitanga, which focuses on continued restoration and enhancement of our environment. theremarkables.co.nz/mountain
3.67m per annum A dedicated, scheduled bus service operates throughout the (excl. snowmaking). day from central Queenstown before 9am and Frankton after 9.30am.
24km, 45min from Queenstown (9km sealed and 4km unsealed access road). TARGETED OPENING DATE 13 June TERRAIN 30% beginner, 40% intermediate, ANTICIPATED CLOSING DATE 11 October 30% advanced.

Calypso, Mid Steeps, and terrain parks. FOOD & BEVERAGE Restaurant and bar services, cafe, Aperol Ice Bar and Barista Bar.

The Remarkables belongs to family owned and SUPPORT FOR CONSERVATION: operated business – NZSki. Our sister mountains are Planting, revegetation and pest control targets remain in across the valley at Coronet Peak and further north at place at each mountain, and we now have some AI traps Methven’s Mt Hutt. in use. In 2025 we began supporting Southern Lakes Sanctuary alongside our work with the Kea At NZSki our primary greenhouse gas emission sources Conservation Trust. are diesel fuel - used for grooming, transport and building heating - and electricity. Together our three mountains produce approximately 3000 tonnes of RENEWABLE ELECTRICITY: greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1 and 2 only) annually. We’ve partnered with Meridian Energy to use their Certified Renewable Energy product. This allows us to report our mountain market-based Scope 2 emissions

In 2024, we introduced Sorting Stations to our food/ We’ve purchased carbon credits from Climate Action drink outlets, reducing waste to landfill by 42% through Company (formerly Carbonz). Since 2022 we have better recycling and reusable tableware. With continued purchased 1336 tonnes of carbon credits to offset use in 2025, we saved 207k units of single-use food emissions. trays from landfill. *Using the market-based reporting methodology as per the GHG Protocol’s Scope 2 Guidance. To find out more visit our Sustainable Slopes page.
